title: ardisia marginata
intro: Ardisia marginata, also known as Red Bells or Coral Bells, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Myrsinaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ardisia marginata include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting immune function, making it a valuable herb in treating various ailments. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and dried extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
